parameters.name: postgres_named_query
parameters.description: Call this tool with ONE input — a single string that is EXACTLY one of these 8 query names (do not wrap it in JSON or any object, just send the raw string): style_profile_get | recent_audit_log | pending_actions_open | executions_summary_24h | spend_total | conversation_transcript | tool_trace_recent | claim_actual_mismatch. The tool returns {ok:true, query_name, rows:[...]} for a valid name, or {ok:false, query_name, error} otherwise. style_profile_get = the operator's learned style profile. recent_audit_log = last 10 bot actions + decisions. pending_actions_open = open HITL-pending actions. executions_summary_24h = n8n executions in last 24h grouped by status. spend_total = the system's cumulative AI/LLM spend in USD (rows[0]: total_usd = authoritative cumulative cost, as_of = last update, logged_usd + log_rows = cross-check from the spend log); use it for 'how much have I spent' / 'how much did the bot/AI cost'. conversation_transcript = the operator's recent Telegram conversation (role + content, newest first). tool_trace_recent = recent tool-call traces (attempt→result: tool_name, status, timings) for claim-vs-actual checks. claim_actual_mismatch = tool calls attempted but never succeeded (silently_dropped/no_success/failed) — the gap between what the bot claimed and what actually ran. Prefer this over the n8n API tool for questions about audit history, pending approvals, conversation style, or execution counts/failures (the API only lists workflows; this query reads the executions table directly).
